summary:
In cases where there are sufficient bone qualities, the AO system recommends using buttress plates instead of more restrictive options due to their ability to share loads effectively without causing unnecessary pressure or damage to healthy tissues. This approach aligns well with minimizing complications while ensuring proper healing through balanced mechanical management strategies as per AO guidelines. To summarize concisely:

When good bone condition exists, use buttress plates according to AO recommendations; they promote efficient force distribution preventing undue stress compared to stiffer alternatives. Key point here involves balancing support vs. potential harm via strategic placement focusing on optimal patient outcomes within defined anatomical contexts guided by established protocols. Agent
To refine this summary into two concise sentences focused only on critical clinical aspects:

In situations with robust bone integrity, the AO system advises against heavy compression techniques, preferring less invasive buttress plates which facilitate better load sharing. These devices help maintain structural stability without exerting harmful pressures typical of stricter fixations when bones can withstand moderate forces naturally. Summary refined now captures essential elements succinctly addressing both technique preference based on bone health status along with rationale behind such choices concerning minimally disruptive care practices endorsed by AO standards.
